user_query:
I'm preparing a short, verifiable reading quiz for our Anne Elizabeth Wilson seminar and need your help creating and validating it from the local materials. Please do all work locally without using the internet.

Inputs (read and use all of these):
- input/texts/AW_Tidal_Library.txt
- input/texts/AW_Margins_of_Winter.txt
- input/reading_list.csv (fields: text_id,title,author,genre,required,target_themes; target_themes are semicolon-separated)
- input/notes.md (contains recommended quotes and themes per text_id)

Task:
1) Write 6 multiple-choice questions (exactly 3 per text_id listed as required in input/reading_list.csv). Save them to outputs/quiz/questions.json as an array of objects with these fields for each question:
   - id: a unique string
   - text_id: must match a text_id from input/reading_list.csv
   - question: the prompt students will see
   - options: array of exactly 4 answer options (strings)
   - answer_index: integer index of the correct option (0=first option, 3=fourth)
   - source_excerpt: an exact substring copied from the corresponding input/texts/<text_id>.txt that the question is grounded in
   - theme_tags: array of one or more tags; every tag must be one of the allowed target_themes for the given text_id (from input/reading_list.csv)

   Requirements for the questions:
   - At least one question for each text_id must use a Quote line from input/notes.md verbatim as its source_excerpt.
   - Each source_excerpt must be an exact substring of its text file and should be sufficient to justify the correct answer.
   - Distribute theme_tags meaningfully based on the content and the allowed target_themes for each text_id.

2) Validate the questions and compute summary statistics:
   - Create outputs/validation/questions_validation.csv with one row per question and these columns: id,text_id,option_count,excerpt_found,excerpt_count,invalid_theme_tags
     • option_count: number of options (should be 4)
     • excerpt_found: true/false whether source_excerpt appears in the source text
     • excerpt_count: how many times source_excerpt appears in the source text (integer)
     • invalid_theme_tags: semicolon-separated tags that are not in the allowed target_themes for that text_id (leave blank if none)
   - Create outputs/validation/summary_stats.json with:
     • total_questions (int)
     • questions_by_text (object mapping text_id to count)
     • avg_question_length_words (number)
     • avg_option_count (number)
     • theme_tag_counts (object mapping tag to total uses across all questions)
     • required_readings_without_questions (array of text_id where required=="yes" and count==0)
     • all_excerpts_found (true if all excerpt_found==true, else false)

3) Draft an email to my seminar instructor summarizing the quiz and validation results. Save it to outputs/message/email_draft.txt. Include a clear subject line and a brief body that:
   - States the number of questions created and coverage by text_id.
   - Reports all_excerpts_found and lists any required_readings_without_questions (or notes that there are none).
   - Mentions key stats (avg_question_length_words, avg_option_count, and top 2 theme tags by count) from outputs/validation/summary_stats.json.
   - References the output file paths you produced.

Notes:
- Use only the provided inputs. Do not fetch external content.
- Ensure every required reading (required=="yes" in input/reading_list.csv) has at least one question.
- The task is complete when the three output files exist and are consistent with each other and the inputs:
  • outputs/quiz/questions.json
  • outputs/validation/questions_validation.csv
  • outputs/validation/summary_stats.json
  • outputs/message/email_draft.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
